evidence_quotes.quote: When computer games began,one would buy a copie in a store.

paragraphs.text: Hello, I really hope that this Act will be revied and eventually pass on, as the situation it was made for, will over time just get worse and worse for the customers. When computer games began,one would buy a copie in a store. Install it on his PC. And play. Over time, through the magic of the internet, if an game had a flaw, a bug or the developers had an update in any form, there was now the possiblity to download it over the internet. And over time, the possibilityof multiplayers without any direct connection over lan was also added. Nowadays, buying a cd in the times of Steam almost seems old school. Many games have an active online connection for downloads, patches, updates and much more,while multiplayer in somce cases isthe only option. Now, this takes time to develope and even more time to keep the servicefor this online product, with this comes costs for the producers, if it is keept active. And so, it is absolutly understandable that at some point the producers decide to no longer invest any money and time in the product and with that shut it it down. Now, this would be absolutley no problem, if there were clear standards for this. What about players who invest money in especially this online product? Who invested time and their money in a product, that was suddenlystopped without the possiblity to get an refund or a way to keep theire product? This is why we have to find a way for players, consumers to be protected,while giving the producers a legal background on which the can safely do buisness. Thanks for your time and have a good day. Sven Maschler
